Tragacanth is a type of dried, gummy sap extracted from the stems of various species of Astragalus, a genus of legumes. The sap is used as a thickening agent and has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and acting as an expectorant to help relieve coughs.

The bioactive constituents of Tragacanth include polysaccharides, mucilages, and various glycosides. These compounds contribute to its medicinal properties and are responsible for its thickening and emulsifying capabilities.

Tragacanth is commonly used in herbal preparations, including capsules, tinctures, and teas, to leverage its medicinal properties. It is also used as an ingredient in various pharmaceutical products, such as cough syrups and digestive aids.
